_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Identify and address cyber workforce planning and management issues (e.g. recruitment, retention, and training).
reference_document_element_description: Conduct data and AI workforce planning, including current-state skills assessment, future-state requirements modeling, gap analysis, and talent acquisition and development strategy formulation.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI LS domain. LS-T-006 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: LS-K-009 (LS, cosine: 0.499), OP-T-004 (OP, cosine: 0.432).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.2/10 (raw 0.418). Shared vocabulary: [planning, workforce]. DCWF-only terms [address, cyber, identify, issues, management, recruitment] vs WIDAI-only terms [acquisition, ai, analysis, assessment, conduct, current] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.883 (P=0.897, R=0.870). P exceeds R by 0.028 — the DCWF element's 14 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 22 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: mixed signal (E:4.9%, N:65.9%, C:29.2%). No dominant classification for "Identify and address cyber workforce planning and management issues (e.g. recrui" vs "Conduct data and AI workforce planning, including current-state skills assessmen" — the relationship is genuinely ambiguous from a logical inference perspective.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.528 > STS 0.418 (gap +0.110). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [planning, workforce] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 9.7%. Shared tokens: [planning, workforce]. DCWF-only: [address, cyber, identify, issues, management, recruitment]. WIDAI-only: [acquisition, ai, analysis, assessment, conduct, current].
